---
title: "ROBINS-I self-assessment"
---

Risk of Bias In Non-randomised Studies of Interventions (ROBINS-I) tool,
applied to this observational analysis.

| Domain | Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Confounding | Moderate | Propensity-score matching + IPW; DAG-derived adjustment set; E-value reporting |
| Selection of participants | Low | Single inclusion criterion (idiopathic PD + NP1PAIN) applied uniformly |
| Classification of interventions | Low | DBS is a discrete, recorded event |
| Deviations from intended interventions | Low | Per-protocol framing in TTE; no protocol deviation in observational data |
| Missing data | Moderate | IPCW + tipping-point MNAR sensitivity (Sprint 3) |
| Measurement of outcomes | Moderate | NP1PAIN is a single 0–4 ordinal item; replicate in future with KPPS / BPI |
| Selection of reported result | Low | Pre-specified primary + 9 documented sprints in PRE_REGISTRATION.md |

**Overall**: Moderate risk of bias, principally from residual confounding
and outcome measurement granularity. The conclusions are presented with
appropriate caveats throughout.
